title: Grafana Annotation Creation Rate for Deployment Correlation
dataSources: `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:audit` annotation create/update, or API-ingested annotation events
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:audit"
| search annotation OR "tags=*deploy*"
| eval deploy_tag=if(match(lower(tags),"(?i)deploy|release|rollback"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as anno_rate, sum(deploy_tag) as deploy_markers by _time, dashboard_uid, org_id
| eventstats median(anno_rate) as med_rate by dashboard_uid
| where anno_rate > med_rate * 4 AND anno_rate > 10
| sort -anno_rate
description: Annotation storms often coincide with flapping CI jobs or runaway automation posting duplicate deployment markers, which obscures incident timelines. Spikes in annotation rate per dashboard highlight misconfigured pipelines before charts become unreadable.
value: Keeps Grafana timelines trustworthy for blameless postmortems and customer communications.
implementation: Normalize `dashboard_uid` from audit payloads. If annotations arrive via custom HEC, set `sourcetype=grafana:audit` for consistency. Exclude synthetic monitors using a tag filter.
visualization: Timechart (annotations/hour), breakdown by `user` or `service`, table of dashboards with spikes.
grandmaExplanation: We watch our primary metrics and dashboard platform for query errors, slow views, and changes that could break the screens teams use every day.
knownFalsePositives: Noisy periods after retention, target, or scrape changes. We compare with the Grafana or Prometheus view for the same minutes.
